strategy: PyMuPDF text + table extraction -> agentic chunking via Gemini

pages.text:
Bayesianbodybuilding.com

Personal Training Client Application Form

The more information you provide, the better I will be able to assist you. If you have
any questions with regards to this form, please do not hesitate to contact me. You
can use whichever measurement system (e.g. metric, imperial) you prefer to fill in
this form. Just state which unit you used (e.g. cm, kg, lb, inch).

 Full legal name: [female]
 Full billing address:
 Age: 26
 Height: 165cm
 Weight: 89kg
 Body fat percentage (plus measurement technique):
 Years of training experience: 0
We need to estimate her body fat percentage, but we don't have pictures or caliper
measurements. She's untrained, so we can use BMI, which is 32.7. Then we need to
conver that to a body fat percentage. [1].
Adult Body Fat % = (1.20 * 32.7 ) + (0.23 * 26) - (10.8 * 0) - 5.4 = 39.82
1: Body mass index as a measure of body fatness: age- and sex-specific prediction
formulas. Authors: Deurenberg P, Weststrate JA, Seidell JC. Journal: Br J Nutr, Mar
1991;65(2):105-14
For the section below, please give your current maximum abilities in weight times
reps, e.g. 250 lb x 6.
 Bench press:
 Squat (excluding body weight):
 Chin-up (excluding body weight):
 Overhead press:

What are your fitness goals?
Lose weight en stay at that weight (60-65kg goal)

This is good, because she should lose fat. Lots of it and quickly. At her ideal weight
she still won't be lean in fitness terms, but compliance wise, it's probably best not to
tell her that yet.

How often in a week would you be prepared to train? Note that no session should last
longer than an hour; 2 sessions is maintenance, 3 is doable and twice daily would
be the absolute maximum at which more is no longer better.
3 times a week prefereably in the morning

Bayesianbodybuilding.com

It is unfortunate that she wants to train in the morning because this is not optimal
(more on this later). However, this does tell us she likely has good sleeping habits.
Only people that sleep enough and at regular times prefer to train in the morning. Or
they think they should. In any case, I would probably let her train in the morning
because this client screams 'compliance problems'.

What is your occupation? You may be general here, e.g. student/manager/personal
trainer/manual labor/doctor. The aim is to characterize your circadian rhythm,
stress and activity level.
Im preparing events (owner)

This means she has a high stress level. Organizing events is stressful and being a
company's owner requires responsibility, which is stressful too. We'll get to the
effects of stress in more detail later.

Please list any pathologies and injuries (disabilities, allergies, illnesses, syndromes,
disorders etc.) you have or had:

Do you plan to use any PEDs while under my supervision? If so, please list them:

Note that if you answer 'NO' to any of the questions below, I will not be able to take
you on as a client.
 I declare that, while Menno Henselmans is my personal trainer, all the
substances I use are obtained and used in accordance with their governing
laws: YES / NO (delete one)
 I forego all rights to hold Menno Henselmans liable for any damages caused in
relation to following his advice as my trainer: YES / NO (delete one)

At this point, you may choose to return this form to me to obtain a price
quote before filling in the rest. Please return the filled in form to
mennohenselmans@bayesianbodybuilding.com and attach at least two full-body
pictures of yourself in clear lighting, preferably including a back double biceps and
front relaxed pose.

Activity level: please mark one of the fields below or delete the rest.
 Sedentary (e.g. desk job) but also doing groceries.
Activity factor = 1
headings: At this point, you may choose to return this form to me to obtain a price
quote before filling in the rest. Please return the filled in form to

pages.text:
Bayesianbodybuilding.com

Stress level: please mark one of the fields below or delete the rest.
 Average stress (e.g. full-time work with deadlines and commuting)

If you answered yes to the question on PED usage, you can leave the section below
blank.
 Wrist circumference:
 Ankle circumference:
 How much did you weigh before you started lifting and what was your height
at that point?
 Were you heavy as a baby?
 Please attach a picture to this form in which the back of your hand is clearly
visible (see example image below).

What are the lightest barbell weight plates you have access to?
1kg

What are the smallest weight increments between the sets of dumbbells you have
access to?
1kg

Do you have access to…
a squat cage or rack? yes
a 45° hyperextension bench? no
a reverse hyper machine? no
a glute-ham raise? yes
a standing calf raise machine? yes
a dip/chin-up belt? no
a leg curl machine and if so, is it seated, lying or standing? Yes seated
a pair of knee wraps? no
a seated calf raise where you push your body up (closed chain: see example image
below)? no

Bayesianbodybuilding.com

Please list all the supplements you are currently taking:

non

Please describe or attach your current diet plan in detail:

Morning: 2 slices of volkoren bread with cheese, ontbijtkoek 1 piece
Crackers
Afternoon: bread with cheese, crackers
Dinner, usually some meat with rice and vegetables
I don't eat/like beef, milk, yoghurt

Typical Dutch diet. This means she likely isn't very educated about nutrition and it
will require a lot of education to stop her from eating a high carb diet. It is also highly
unlikely that this is in fact her typical diet, because she wouldn't have become obese if
it was. This signals that underreporting is likely to become an issue.

It is reasonable to estimate her thermic effect of food as 10%, since that is the average
in research.

Please describe or attach your current training program in detail: non

Are there any times at which you are unable or unwilling to train? If so, please list
them:
In the afternoon.

Please return the filled in form to mennohenselmans@bayesianbodybuilding.com
and attach at least two full-body pictures of yourself in clear lighting (without
excessive shadows), preferably including a back double biceps and front relaxed
pose. If you recently had blood work done, you can attach its results as well.

Bayesianbodybuilding.com

We have everything we need to calculate her macros now. However, since she is
obese, the optimal diet will be a protein sparing modified fast (PSMF).

1.8 g/kg will have her protein intake at 160 g/d. That's probably excessive, but since
she is a beginner, she should still be able to build muscle very rapidly even on a
PSMF, so I wouldn't lower it.

Then we need to evaluate the need for fats and carbs. Given her lifestyle, undoubtedly
horrific carb tolerance due to pre-metabolic syndrome and low training frequency,
the carb requirement is essentially zero. However, given the likelihood of compliance
issues and the unlikeliness of hunger being a main problem due to her high body fat
percentage, I wouldn't recommend a ketogenic diet, at least not to start with.

Fats are beneficial because her diet is severely lacking in nutritious fats. With a REE
of 1680, her optimal minimum fat intake would be 75 g/d. Since her optimal deficit is
> 50%, that's impossible, so I would recommend the absolute minimum of fats and
carbs. In practice, I then often set carbs at half of protein and fat at a quarter of
protein.

Protein Intake
160
Fat Intake
40
Carbohydrate Intake 80
Kcal
1321

tables.markdown:
| Protein Intake | 160 |
| --- | --- |
| Fat Intake | 40 |
| Carbohydrate Intake | 80 |
| Kcal | 1321 |
